links.title: F2LLM-v2: Inclusive, Performant, and Efficient Embeddings for a Multilingual World

primary_link.title: F2LLM-v2: Inclusive, Performant, and Efficient Embeddings for a Multilingual World

papers.summary: We present F2LLM-v2, a new family of general-purpose, multilingual embedding models in 8 distinct sizes ranging from 80M to 14B. Trained on a newly curated composite of 60 million publicly available high-quality data samples, F2LLM-v2 supports more than 200 languages, with a particular emphasis on previously underserved mid- and low-resource languages. By integrating a two-stage LLM-based embedding training pipeline with matryoshka learning, model pruning, and knowledge distillation techniques, we present models that are far more efficient than previous LLM-based embedding models while retaining competitive performances. Extensive evaluations confirm that F2LLM-v2-14B ranks first on 11 MTEB benchmarks, while the smaller models in the family also set a new state of the art for resource-constrained applications. To facilitate open-source embedding model research, we release all models, data, code, and intermediate checkpoints.
papers.summary_zh: 我們提出了 F2LLM-v2，這是一個新的通用多語言 embedding 模型家族，共有 8 種不同尺寸，範圍從 80M 到 14B。F2LLM-v2 透過對新策劃的 6000 萬個公開高品質數據樣本進行訓練，支援超過 200 種語言，特別強調了先前服務不足的中低資源語言。透過將兩階段的 LLM-based embedding 訓練流程與 matryoshka learning、model pruning 和 knowledge distillation 技術結合，我們提出了比以前的 LLM-based embedding 模型更有效率，同時保持競爭力表現的模型。廣泛的評估證實 F2LLM-v2-14B 在 11 個 MTEB benchmarks 上排名第一，而該家族中較小的模型也為資源受限的應用樹立了新的 state of the art。為了促進開源 embedding 模型研究，我們發布了所有模型、數據、程式碼和中間檢查點。

links.title: Generation Models Know Space: Unleashing Implicit 3D Priors for Scene Understanding

primary_link.title: Generation Models Know Space: Unleashing Implicit 3D Priors for Scene Understanding

papers.summary: While Multimodal Large Language Models demonstrate impressive semantic capabilities, they often suffer from spatial blindness, struggling with fine-grained geometric reasoning and physical dynamics. Existing solutions typically rely on explicit 3D modalities or complex geometric scaffolding, which are limited by data scarcity and generalization challenges. In this work, we propose a paradigm shift by leveraging the implicit spatial prior within large-scale video generation models. We posit that to synthesize temporally coherent videos, these models inherently learn robust 3D structural priors and physical laws. We introduce VEGA-3D (Video Extracted Generative Awareness), a plug-and-play framework that repurposes a pre-trained video diffusion model as a Latent World Simulator. By extracting spatiotemporal features from intermediate noise levels and integrating them with semantic representations via a token-level adaptive gated fusion mechanism, we enrich MLLMs with dense geometric cues without explicit 3D supervision. Extensive experiments across 3D scene understanding, spatial reasoning, and embodied manipulation benchmarks demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, validating that generative priors provide a scalable foundation for physical-world understanding. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/H-EmbodVis/VEGA-3D.
papers.summary_zh: 儘管 Multimodal Large Language Models (多模態大型語言模型) 展現出令人印象深刻的語義能力，它們卻常常面臨空間盲點，難以進行細粒度的幾何推理和物理動力學分析。現有解決方案通常依賴於顯式的 3D 模態或複雜的幾何支架，這受限於數據稀缺性和泛化挑戰。在這項工作中，我們提出了一種範式轉變，利用大規模 video generation models 中的隱式空間先驗。我們認為，為了合成時間上連貫的影片，這些模型本質上會學習到穩健的 3D 結構先驗和物理定律。我們引入了 VEGA-3D (Video Extracted Generative Awareness)，這是一個即插即用的框架，它將一個預訓練的 video diffusion model 重新用作 Latent World Simulator。透過從中間 noise levels 中提取時空特徵，並透過 token-level adaptive gated fusion mechanism 將其與語義表示整合，我們在沒有顯式 3D 監督的情況下，為 MLLMs 提供了豐富的密集幾何線索。跨 3D scene understanding、spatial reasoning 和 embodied manipulation benchmarks 的廣泛實驗表明，我們的方法優於現有的 state-of-the-art baseline，驗證了 generative priors 為物理世界理解提供了可擴展的基礎。程式碼可在 https://github.com/H-EmbodVis/VEGA-3D 公開獲取。
papers.title: Generation Models Know Space: Unleashing Implicit 3D Priors for Scene Understanding

links.title: FASTER: Rethinking Real-Time Flow VLAs

primary_link.title: FASTER: Rethinking Real-Time Flow VLAs

papers.summary: Real-time execution is crucial for deploying Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models in the physical world. Existing asynchronous inference methods primarily optimize trajectory smoothness, but neglect the critical latency in reacting to environmental changes. By rethinking the notion of reaction in action chunking policies, this paper presents a systematic analysis of the factors governing reaction time. We show that reaction time follows a uniform distribution determined jointly by the Time to First Action (TTFA) and the execution horizon. Moreover, we reveal that the standard practice of applying a constant schedule in flow-based VLAs can be inefficient and forces the system to complete all sampling steps before any movement can start, forming the bottleneck in reaction latency. To overcome this issue, we propose Fast Action Sampling for ImmediaTE Reaction (FASTER). By introducing a Horizon-Aware Schedule, FASTER adaptively prioritizes near-term actions during flow sampling, compressing the denoising of the immediate reaction by tenfold (e.g., in π_{0.5} and X-VLA) into a single step, while preserving the quality of long-horizon trajectory. Coupled with a streaming client-server pipeline, FASTER substantially reduces the effective reaction latency on real robots, especially when deployed on consumer-grade GPUs. Real-world experiments, including a highly dynamic table tennis task, prove that FASTER unlocks unprecedented real-time responsiveness for generalist policies, enabling rapid generation of accurate and smooth trajectories.

primary_link.title: Perceptio: Perception Enhanced Vision Language Models via Spatial Token Generation

radar.summary: Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) excel at semantic understanding but struggle with fine grained spatial grounding, as the model must implicitly infer complex geometry without ever producing a spatial interpretation. Concretely, we (i) distill a VQVAE depth codebook from a strong m
radar.summary_zh: 大型視覺語言模型 (LVLMs) 在語義理解方面表現出色，但在細粒度空間定位上卻面臨困難，因為模型必須隱式推斷複雜幾何形狀，而從未產生空間解釋。我們提出了 Perceptio，這是一個具備感知增強能力的 LVLM，透過在自回歸序列中直接生成明確的語義分割 tokens 和深度 tokens，實現了 2D 和 3D 空間推理能力。具體而言，我們 (i) 從一個強大的模型中蒸餾出 VQVAE 深度碼本。
radar.title: Perceptio: Perception Enhanced Vision Language Models via Spatial Token Generation

links.title: Mi:dm K 2.5 Pro

primary_link.title: Mi:dm K 2.5 Pro

radar.summary:
The evolving LLM landscape requires capabilities beyond simple text generation, prioritizing multi-step reasoning, long-context understanding, and agentic workflows. This shift challenges existing models in enterprise environments, especially in Korean-language and domain-specific scenarios where scaling is insufficient. We introduce Mi:dm K 2.5 Pro, a 32B parameter flagship LLM designed to address enterprise-grade complexity through reasoning-focused optimization.
  Our methodology builds a rob
radar.summary_zh: 不斷發展的 LLM 領域需要超越簡單文本生成的能力，優先考慮多步驟推理、長上下文理解和智能體工作流程。這一轉變對企業環境中的現有模型構成了挑戰，尤其是在韓語和領域特定場景中，擴展性不足。我們引入了 Mi:dm K 2.5 Pro，這是一個 320億參數的旗艦級 LLM，旨在透過以推理為中心的優化來解決企業級複雜性。我們的方法建立了一個強固的基礎。
radar.title: Mi:dm K 2.5 Pro
radar.title_zh: Mi:dm K 2.5 Pro

links.title: A Concept is More Than a Word: Diversified Unlearning in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models

primary_link.title: A Concept is More Than a Word: Diversified Unlearning in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models

radar.summary: Concept unlearning has emerged as a promising direction for reducing the risks of harmful content generation in text-to-image diffusion models by selectively erasing undesirable concepts from a model's parameters. radar.summary_zh: 概念遺忘已成為一個有前景的方向，透過選擇性地抹除模型參數中不需要的概念，來降低 Text-to-Image Diffusion Models 生成有害內容的風險。現有方法通常依賴關鍵字來識別待遺忘的目標概念。然而，我們發現這種基於關鍵字的方法本質上是受限的：一個視覺概念是多維的，可以以多樣的文本形式表達，並且經常與相關概念重疊。
radar.title: A Concept is More Than a Word: Diversified Unlearning in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
radar.title_zh: 概念不僅僅是一個詞：Text-to-Image Diffusion Models 中的多樣化概念遺忘

links.title: Automatic detection of Gen-AI texts: A comparative framework of neural models

primary_link.title: Automatic detection of Gen-AI texts: A comparative framework of neural models

radar.summary: The rapid proliferation of Large Language Models has significantly increased the difficulty of distinguishing between human-written and AI generated texts, raising critical issues across academic, editorial, and social domains. radar.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (Large Language Models) 的迅速普及顯著增加了區分人類撰寫和 AI 生成文本的難度，在學術、編輯和社會領域引發了關鍵問題。本文透過設計、實施和比較評估多個基於機器學習的檢測器，研究了 AI 生成文本檢測問題。開發並分析了四種神經架構：Multilayer Perceptron、一個一維的...

primary_link.title: Measuring and Exploiting Confirmation Bias in LLM-Assisted Security Code Review

radar.summary:
Security code reviews increasingly rely on systems integrating Large Language Models (LLMs), ranging from interactive assistants to autonomous agents in CI/CD pipelines. radar.summary_zh: 安全程式碼審查越來越依賴於整合 Large Language Models (LLMs) 的系統，從互動式助理到 CI/CD 管道中的自主代理。我們研究了確認偏誤（即傾向於支持與先前預期一致的解釋）是否會影響基於 LLM 的漏洞檢測，以及這種失敗模式是否可以在軟體供應鏈攻擊中被利用。我們進行了兩項互補研究。研究 1 透過對照組量化了確認偏誤。

primary_link.title: A Comparative Empirical Study of Catastrophic Forgetting Mitigation in Sequential Task Adaptation for Continual Natural Language Processing Systems

links.title: Reasoning over mathematical objects: on-policy reward modeling and test time aggregation

primary_link.title: Reasoning over mathematical objects: on-policy reward modeling and test time aggregation

top5.summary: The ability to precisely derive mathematical objects is a core requirement for downstream STEM applications, including mathematics, physics, and chemistry, where reasoning must culminate in formally structured expressions. Yet, current LM evaluations of mathematical and scientific reasoning rely heavily on simplified answer formats such as numerical values or multiple choice options due to the convenience of automated assessment. In this paper we provide three contributions for improving reasoning over mathematical objects: (i) we build and release training data and benchmarks for deriving mathematical objects, the Principia suite; (ii) we provide training recipes with strong LLM-judges and verifiers, where we show that on-policy judge training boosts performance; (iii) we show how on-policy training can also be used to scale test-time compute via aggregation. We find that strong LMs such as Qwen3-235B and o3 struggle on Principia, while our training recipes can bring significant improvements over different LLM backbones, while simultaneously improving results on existing numerical and MCQA tasks, demonstrating cross-format generalization of reasoning abilities.
top5.summary_zh: 精確推導數學物件是下游 STEM 應用（包括數學、物理和化學）的核心要求，其中推理必須最終形成形式化結構表達。然而，當前 LM 對數學和科學推理的評估，由於自動評估的便利性，過於依賴簡化的答案格式，例如數值或多重選擇。在本文中，我們為改進數學物件的推理提供了三項貢獻：(i) 我們建立並發布了用於推導數學物件的訓練資料和基準，即 Principia suite；(ii) 我們提供了使用強大 LLM 評審和驗證器的訓練方法，其中我們展示了 on-policy 評審訓練能提升性能；(iii) 我們展示了 on-policy 訓練如何也能透過聚合來擴展測試時計算。我們發現強大的 LM，例如 Qwen3-235B 和 o3 在 Principia 上表現不佳，而我們的訓練方法可以在不同的 LLM 骨幹上帶來顯著改進，同時改進現有數值和 MCQA 任務的結果，展示了推理能力的跨格式泛化。
top5.title: Reasoning over mathematical objects: on-policy reward modeling and test time aggregation
top5.title_zh: 對數學物件的推理：on-policy 獎勵建模與測試時聚合

links.title: Nemotron-Cascade 2: Post-Training LLMs with Cascade RL and Multi-Domain On-Policy Distillation

primary_link.title: Nemotron-Cascade 2: Post-Training LLMs with Cascade RL and Multi-Domain On-Policy Distillation

top5.summary: We introduce Nemotron-Cascade 2, an open 30B MoE model with 3B activated parameters that delivers best-in-class reasoning and strong agentic capabilities. Despite its compact size, its mathematical and coding reasoning performance approaches that of frontier open models. It is the second open-weight LLM, after DeepSeekV3.2-Speciale-671B-A37B, to achieve Gold Medal-level performance in the 2025 International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO), the International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI), and the ICPC World Finals, demonstrating remarkably high intelligence density with 20x fewer parameters. In contrast to Nemotron-Cascade 1, the key technical advancements are as follows. After SFT on a meticulously curated dataset, we substantially expand Cascade RL to cover a much broader spectrum of reasoning and agentic domains. Furthermore, we introduce multi-domain on-policy distillation from the strongest intermediate teacher models for each domain throughout the Cascade RL process, allowing us to efficiently recover benchmark regressions and sustain strong performance gains along the way. We release the collection of model checkpoint and training data.
top5.summary_zh: 我們推出了 Nemotron-Cascade 2，這是一個開放的 30B MoE 模型，具有 3B 啟用參數，提供了一流的推理和強大的代理能力。儘管尺寸緊湊，其數學和編碼推理性能已接近頂尖的開源模型。它是繼 DeepSeekV3.2-Speciale-671B-A37B 之後第二個開源 LLM，在 2025 年國際數學奧林匹亞 (IMO)、國際資訊學奧林匹亞 (IOI) 和 ICPC 全球總決賽中取得金牌級表現，以減少 20 倍的參數展示了驚人的高智慧密度。與 Nemotron-Cascade 1 相比，主要的技術進步如下。在對精心策劃的資料集進行 SFT 後，我們大幅擴展了 Cascade RL，使其涵蓋更廣泛的推理和代理領域。此外，我們在整個 Cascade RL 過程中，從每個領域最強大的中間教師模型引入了多領域 on-policy 蒸餾，使我們能夠有效恢復基準回歸並在此過程中維持強勁的性能增益。我們發布了模型檢查點和訓練資料的集合。
top5.title: Nemotron-Cascade 2: Post-Training LLMs with Cascade RL and Multi-Domain On-Policy Distillation
top5.title_zh: Nemotron-Cascade 2：透過 Cascade RL 和多領域 On-Policy 蒸餾對 LLM 進行後訓練

links.title: ProRL Agent: Rollout-as-a-Service for RL Training of Multi-Turn LLM Agents

primary_link.title: ProRL Agent: Rollout-as-a-Service for RL Training of Multi-Turn LLM Agents

top5.summary: Multi-turn LLM agents are increasingly important for solving complex, interactive tasks, and reinforcement learning (RL) is a key ingredient for improving their long-horizon behavior. However, RL training requires generating large numbers of sandboxed rollout trajectories, and existing infrastructures often couple rollout orchestration with the training loop, making systems hard to migrate and maintain. Under the rollout-as-a-service philosophy, we present ProRL Agent , a scalable infrastructure that serves the full agentic rollout lifecycle through an API service. ProRL Agent also provides standardized and extensible sandbox environments that support diverse agentic tasks in rootless HPC settings. We validate ProRL Agent through RL training on software engineering, math, STEM, and coding tasks. ProRL Agent is open-sourced and integrated as part of NVIDIA NeMo Gym.
top5.summary_zh: 多輪 LLM 代理對於解決複雜的互動式任務越來越重要，而強化學習 (RL) 是改進其長程行為的關鍵要素。然而，RL 訓練需要生成大量的沙盒化 rollout 軌跡，而現有基礎設施通常將 rollout 編排與訓練迴圈耦合，使得系統難以遷移和維護。秉持 rollout 即服務的理念，我們提出了 ProRL Agent，這是一個可擴展的基礎設施，透過 API 服務為完整的代理 rollout 生命週期提供支援。ProRL Agent 還提供標準化且可擴展的沙盒環境，可在無根 HPC 環境中支援多樣化的代理任務。我們透過在軟體工程、數學、STEM 和編碼任務上的 RL 訓練來驗證 ProRL Agent。ProRL Agent 已開源，並整合為 NVIDIA NeMo Gym 的一部分。
top5.title: ProRL Agent: Rollout-as-a-Service for RL Training of Multi-Turn LLM Agents
top5.title_zh: ProRL Agent：用於多輪 LLM 代理的 RL 訓練之 Rollout 即服務

links.title: Memento-Skills: Let Agents Design Agents

primary_link.title: Memento-Skills: Let Agents Design Agents

top5.summary:
We introduce Memento-Skills, a generalist, continually-learnable LLM agent system that functions as an agent-designing agent: it autonomously constructs, adapts, and improves task-specific agents through experience. The system is built on a memory-based reinforcement learning framework with stateful prompts, where reusable skills (stored as structured markdown files) serve as persistent, evolving memory. These skills encode both behaviour and context, enabling the agent to carry forward knowledge across interactions.
  Starting from simple elementary skills (like Web search and terminal operations), the agent continually improves via the Read--Write Reflective Learning mechanism introduced in Memento~2~wang2025memento2. In the read phase, a behaviour-trainable skill router selects the most relevant skill conditioned on the current stateful prompt; in the write phase, the agent updates and expands its skill library based on new experience. This closed-loop design enables continual learning without updating LLM parameters, as all adaptation is realised through the evolution of externalised skills and prompts.
  Unlike prior approaches that rely on human-designed agents, Memento-Skills enables a generalist agent to design agents end-to-end for new tasks. Through iterative skill generation and refinement, the system progressively improves its own capabilities. Experiments on the General AI Assistants benchmark and Humanity's Last Exam demonstrate sustained gains, achieving 26.2\% and 116.2\% relative improvements in overall accuracy, respectively. Code is available at https://github.com/Memento-Teams/Memento-Skills.
top5.summary_zh:
我們介紹了 Memento-Skills，這是一個通用型、可持續學習的 LLM 代理系統，其功能類似於一個代理設計代理：它透過經驗自主建構、調整和改進任務特定代理。該系統建立在一個基於記憶的強化學習框架上，並結合了有狀態提示，其中可重複使用的技能（儲存為結構化的 Markdown 文件）充當持久且演進的記憶。這些技能編碼了行為和上下文，使代理能夠在不同互動中傳遞知識。
從簡單的基本技能（如網路搜尋和終端操作）開始，代理透過 Memento~2~wang2025memento2 中引入的讀寫反射學習機制持續改進。在讀取階段，行為可訓練的技能路由器根據當前的有狀態提示選擇最相關的技能；在寫入階段，代理根據新經驗更新和擴展其技能庫。這種閉環設計實現了持續學習，而無需更新 LLM 參數，因為所有適應都是透過外部化技能和提示的演進實現的。
與依賴人類設計代理的先前方法不同，Memento-Skills 使通用型代理能夠為新任務端到端地設計代理。透過迭代技能生成和完善，系統逐步提升自身能力。在 General AI Assistants 基準和 Humanity's Last Exam 上的實驗表明持續的增益，總體準確度分別達到了 26.2% 和 116.2% 的相對改進。程式碼可在 https://github.com/Memento-Teams/Memento-Skills 獲取。
top5.title: Memento-Skills: Let Agents Design Agents

links.title: LVOmniBench: Pioneering Long Audio-Video Understanding Evaluation for Omnimodal LLMs

primary_link.title: LVOmniBench: Pioneering Long Audio-Video Understanding Evaluation for Omnimodal LLMs

top5.summary: Recent advancements in omnimodal large language models (OmniLLMs) have significantly improved the comprehension of audio and video inputs. However, current evaluations primarily focus on short audio and video clips ranging from 10 seconds to 5 minutes, failing to reflect the demands of real-world applications, where videos typically run for tens of minutes. To address this critical gap, we introduce LVOmniBench, a new benchmark designed specifically for the cross-modal comprehension of long-form audio and video. This dataset comprises high-quality videos sourced from open platforms that feature rich audio-visual dynamics. Through rigorous manual selection and annotation, LVOmniBench comprises 275 videos, ranging in duration from 10 to 90 minutes, and 1,014 question-answer (QA) pairs. LVOmniBench aims to rigorously evaluate the capabilities of OmniLLMs across domains, including long-term memory, temporal localization, fine-grained understanding, and multimodal perception. Our extensive evaluation reveals that current OmniLLMs encounter significant challenges when processing extended audio-visual inputs. Open-source models generally achieve accuracies below 35%, whereas the Gemini 3 Pro reaches a peak accuracy of approximately 65%. We anticipate that this dataset, along with our empirical findings, will stimulate further research and the development of advanced models capable of resolving complex cross-modal understanding problems within long-form audio-visual contexts.
top5.summary_zh: 全模態大型語言模型 (OmniLLMs) 的最新進展顯著提升了音訊和視訊輸入的理解能力。然而，目前的評估主要集中在 10 秒到 5 分鐘的短音訊和視訊片段，未能反映現實世界應用的需求，其中視訊通常會持續數十分鐘。為了解決這一關鍵差距，我們引入了 LVOmniBench，這是一個專為長篇音訊和視訊的跨模態理解而設計的新基準。該資料集包含來自開放平台的高品質視訊，具有豐富的音視訊動態。透過嚴格的人工篩選和註釋，LVOmniBench 包含 275 個視訊，時長從 10 到 90 分鐘不等，以及 1,014 個問答 (QA) 對。LVOmniBench 旨在嚴格評估 OmniLLMs 在多個領域的能力，包括長期記憶、時間定位、細粒度理解和多模態感知。我們廣泛的評估顯示，目前的 OmniLLMs 在處理擴展的音視訊輸入時面臨重大挑戰。開源模型通常準確度低於 35%，而 Gemini 3 Pro 達到了約 65% 的峰值準確度。我們預計該資料集以及我們的實證結果，將激發進一步的研究和先進模型的開發，以解決長篇音視訊上下文中的複雜跨模態理解問題。
top5.title: LVOmniBench: Pioneering Long Audio-Video Understanding Evaluation for Omnimodal LLMs
top5.title_zh: LVOmniBench：開創性的多模態 LLM 長音訊-視訊理解評估
